skill.name: ohmg
skill.description: Orchestrates multi-agent development workflows for Google Antigravity projects using oh-my-ag, Serena Memory, and mapped AI CLIs.
skill.summary: Coordinate PM, frontend, backend, QA, and debug agents through oh-my-ag and Serena Memory.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: The external command findings are partially confirmed because the skill instructs users or agents to run oh-my-ag and bunx commands. The network findings are limited to localhost dashboard and bridge examples, and the weak cryptography alerts are false positives with no cryptographic code present. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Instructions
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill tells users or agents to run bunx and oh-my-ag commands. This is expected for setup and orchestration, but it executes external tooling and can change the workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and directly executable. The context supports legitimate setup and agent orchestration, so this is elevated risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Workspace State and Configuration Writes
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill describes writing Serena Memory state, installing skills into .agent/skills, and editing agent CLI mappings. These writes are normal for this workflow but affect project files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file paths and setup behavior are explicitly documented. The risk is bounded because the writes are described as local project configuration and state.

low_findings.title: Localhost Dashboard and Bridge Endpoints
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill references localhost web and SSE endpoints. The URLs are local examples, so the network risk is low unless the user exposes those services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are present and network-related. Both use localhost, which reduces exfiltration concern and supports a benign dashboard or bridge use case.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The reported lines contain a skill description and an output example marker. No cryptographic function, hash operation, or weak algorithm usage appears on those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were reviewed directly and do not contain cryptographic code. The static alert appears to match unrelated text rather than a real algorithm.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Matches Without Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Several static external command alerts point to paths, slash commands, or configuration examples. These are documentation text, not shell execution by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation examples and configuration snippets. They do not invoke a shell unless a separate agent decides to act on them.

dangerous_patterns.title: Package Runner Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs use of bunx to run oh-my-ag commands. This pattern can execute package code from an external ecosystem.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The bunx command examples are direct and executable. They are legitimate setup commands, but they remain a real supply-chain execution pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bridge Command With URL Argument
dangerous_patterns.description: The bridge command accepts a URL argument for a localhost SSE endpoint. This can connect agent tooling to a running local service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command and URL are explicit. The endpoint is localhost, so the risk depends on the user's local environment and service exposure.

content.value_statement: Complex software projects need clear ownership across planning, frontend, backend, QA, and debugging work. This skill guides oh-my-ag orchestration with Serena Memory, dashboards, and CLI mappings.
actual_capabilities: Defines specialized PM, frontend, backend, QA, and debug agent roles.
Provides setup and verification commands for the oh-my-ag CLI.
Documents Serena Memory usage for shared workflow state.
Shows coordination, agent spawn, dashboard, and bridge command patterns.
Provides an example mapping from agent roles to Gemini, Codex, and Claude.
Supports monitoring through terminal and local web dashboard workflows.
limitations: Requires Bun and uv for the documented setup flow.
Depends on the external oh-my-ag CLI and configured AI tools.
Does not include implementation code for agents or dashboards.
Local dashboards and bridges must be secured by the user.

use_cases.title: Plan a Full-Stack Feature
use_cases.description: Break a feature into PM, frontend, backend, and QA work while keeping shared state visible.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Agent Assignments
use_cases.description: Route tasks to specialized agents and map each role to a preferred AI CLI.

use_cases.title: Monitor Multi-Agent Progress
use_cases.description: Use Serena Memory and dashboards to inspect agent status during a complex project.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Coordination
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ohmg skill to explain the setup steps for this repository. Include prerequisites, verification, and files that may change.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Agent Roles
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ohmg skill to split this feature into PM, frontend, backend, QA, and debug responsibilities. Keep the plan concise.

prompt_templates.title: Configure CLI Mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ohmg skill to recommend agent CLI mappings for a team using Claude, Codex, and Gemini. Explain each mapping.

prompt_templates.title: Run an Observed Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ohmg skill to design a monitored multi-agent workflow with Serena Memory updates, dashboard checks, and completion criteria.

output_examples.input: Plan a payment feature with multiple agents.
output: PM agent defines requirements, constraints, and acceptance criteria.
Backend agent designs the payment API and persistence changes.
Frontend agent builds the checkout screens and state handling.
QA agent creates integration and regression checks.

output_examples.input: Prepare this project for oh-my-ag coordination.
output: Run the setup flow after user approval.
Verify installation with the doctor command.
Review generated agent skills and preferences before coordination.
Open the terminal dashboard for live agent status.
Use the local web dashboard when a browser view is needed.
Review Serena Memory entries for shared progress and blockers.

best_practices: Ask for approval before running bunx or oh-my-ag commands.
Review generated .agent and .serena files before committing changes.
Use localhost dashboards only in trusted development environments.
anti_patterns: Do not run external package commands in sensitive repositories without review.
Do not expose local dashboard or bridge ports to untrusted networks.
Do not assign agents broad workspace changes without clear task boundaries.

faq.question: What does this skill help coordinate?
faq.answer: It helps coordinate multi-agent development workflows across planning, frontend, backend, QA, debugging, and monitoring tasks.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: The skill documents commands for setup and orchestration. A user or agent must still choose to execute them.

faq.question: Why is the risk level medium?
faq.answer: The skill uses external CLI commands and local service endpoints. These are legitimate but require user review.

faq.question: Does it require a specific AI tool?
faq.answer: No. It shows mappings for Claude, Codex, and Gemini, and it supports Claude Code compatibility metadata.

faq.question: What files can it affect?
faq.answer: The documented workflow can affect .agent configuration and .serena memory files inside a project.

faq.question: Is the weak cryptography warning confirmed?
faq.answer: No. The cited lines do not contain cryptographic code or weak hash usage.
